decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce the thermal gradient under the chip. A conservative 40% increase is proposed, staying well within the allowed 2x step ratio.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat distribution, lowering the local hot spot temperature on the chip.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions rather than lateral spreading.
High conductivity values may require checking if the material model remains physically realistic for the specific application context.
